title: Fix member login failure

requirements.text: Investigate and fix the member login authentication flow that is currently showing 'failed' errors when members attempt to log in with their membership ID
acceptanceCriteria: Members can successfully log in using their membership ID
Login errors display specific, actionable error messages instead of generic 'failed' status
The useMemberAuth hook properly validates membership IDs against the backend
The MemberLoginPage component handles authentication errors gracefully with clear user feedback

requirements.text: Add error logging and diagnostics to the member authentication flow to capture why login attempts are failing, including backend validation responses and network errors
acceptanceCriteria: Console logs show detailed error information when member login fails
Backend validateMemberLogin function returns specific error codes for different failure scenarios (invalid ID, expired membership, etc.)
Frontend displays user-friendly error messages based on specific failure reasons

requirements.text: Verify that the backend's validateMemberLogin method correctly queries member records and returns appropriate success/failure responses with proper error handling
acceptanceCriteria: validateMemberLogin method properly searches for members by membership ID
The method handles edge cases like non-existent IDs, expired memberships, and inactive members
Appropriate Result types are returned with clear error messages

constraints: Do not modify the useMemberAuth hook's localStorage and cross-tab synchronization logic unless directly related to the login failure
Preserve the existing member authentication flow architecture
Maintain backward compatibility with existing member IDs in the system
nonGoals: Adding new authentication methods beyond membership ID
Modifying admin login or Internet Identity integration
